title: vicia lathyroides subsp. arenivaga
intro: Vicia lathyroides subsp. arenivaga, commonly known as the mountain pea or vetch, is a subspecies of the vetch plant, belonging to the legume family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic, lowering blood pressure and reducing water retention. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits such as relieving joint pain, reducing the risk of infections, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, Vicia lathyroides subsp. arenivaga is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its health benefits.
